According to the Giraffe Conservation Foundation (most recent surveys), there are approximately **117,000 wild giraffes** plus roughly **2,000 in captivity**, giving ~**119,000 total**.

### Step 2: Spots per Giraffe (by species/subspecies)
All four recognized giraffe species (Northern, Reticulated, Masai, Southern) have distinct patch patterns, but similar counts:

| Body Region | Estimated Patches |
|-------------|-------------------|
| Neck | ~25–35 |
| Torso | ~60–80 |
| Legs | ~25–35 |
| Head/face | ~10–15 |
| **Total** | **~130–160** |

Using giraffe individual-identification research (which counts distinct patches), I estimate an average of **~150 patches per giraffe**. Reticulated giraffes have fewer, larger patches (~120); Masai giraffes have more irregular, numerous patches (~170); averaging across populations lands near 150.

### Step 3: Calculation

$$119{,}000 \text{ giraffes} \times 150 \text{ patches/giraffe} = 17{,}850{,}000$$

---

## My Single Best Estimate: **17,850,000**

(roughly 18 million spots)
